//: Example .explicitrc.json. Drop this file at the root of the project you want ExplicitJS to analyze (it walks up from the analyzed path looking for it). Every key is optional; the value shown is the default used when the key is omitted. Command-line flags always override these values, and the two list settings (exclude-type, include-extra) are merged with their --exclude-type / --include-extra counterparts on the CLI.
//exclude-type: Turn checks off entirely. Defaults to [] (nothing excluded). Each value:
if                  - truthiness test in an if:        if (items)
while               - truthiness test in while/do:     while (queue)
ternary             - any inline conditional:          cond ? x : y
ones unless promoted via include-extra below)
single_letter_var   - one-character names: x, n, e
single_use_var      - a variable assigned once and read once
single_use_func     - a function defined once and called once
//include-extra: Opt into stricter variants of the 'exotic' checks. By default these only
flag an ambiguous (implicit-boolean) use; listing a check here flags
EVERY occurrence. Defaults to []. Valid values:
arrow - flag all arrow / function expressions, not just ambiguous ones
